summary: Crisp Charlie Miller soundboard upgrade captures a stellar ’79 show with standout Half‑Step/F​ranklin’s opener, Stella Blue and Mexicali Blues, though some hear occasional bass drop‑outs.
review: The recording is a soundboard capture that many reviewers describe as crisp and well‑balanced after Miller’s master upgrade, despite a few listeners reporting bass cut‑outs that make parts of the set non‑listenable. Musically the show shines with an exciting opener (Half‑Step > Franklin’s Tower), powerful renditions of Stella Blue, Sugaree and Mexicali Blues, and a second‑set that heats up quickly.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 5‑star praising both performance and sound quality; only one low rating mentions a specific bass issue, so overall the show and recording merit a high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp master soundboard upgrade with strong low‑end; some reviewers note heavy bass/drums and occasional bass cut‑out that can render sections non‑listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A compelling opener with Half‑Step > Franklin’s sets the tone; the second set builds quickly, featuring a rare Sugaree placement and finishes strong with high energy.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil's bass is described as heavy and prominent, giving the mix a powerful low‑end feel.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s upper harmony on Mexicali Blues is highlighted as a breakthrough vocal addition.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums are noted as heavy but add to the overall power of the performance.
